attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Pyogenic granuloma (candidate for lobular capillary haemangioma)

transform_flags: Sex stated in the source note (male — the note refers to the patient as 'he'/'his'). Age stated as 8 years. age_band set to 'child'.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Paediatric case (under 18): per the hard limits the patient is flagged for in-person review and NO paediatric drug dose was synthesised. Topical salicylic acid for the remaining warts is recorded as recommended_first_line but with preparation/strength/regimen explicitly deferred to an in-person GP/pharmacist; cryotherapy is mentioned only as a routine option. No dose was invented.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected the lesion, achieved haemostasis with a silver-nitrate chemical cautery stick and applied a firm protective dressing. The chemical-cautery procedure is reprojected to (a) coaching firm sustained direct pressure + elevation the carer can perform and (b) in-person review for cautery/pressure dressing IF pressure fails. Patient/video-observable findings (the rough cauliflower-textured bump, the torn top with an oozing vascular base and black dots, the several smaller adjacent lesions, an alert well-appearing tearful child, blood-stained tissue, parent-reported no fever / no surrounding infection) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false). Clinician-measured vitals and weight (HR 95, BP 105/65, Temp 36.8, RR 18, SpO2 99%, Wt 28 kg) and the in-person cautery are sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header name the diagnosis (warts / verruca vulgaris); the green side (01/02) carries only the lay presentation (rough hard long-standing bumps, a torn top that won't stop bleeding, black dots at the base, several similar bumps) and no diagnosis label. The full primary_diagnosis.name does not appear in any injectable field.

Tier set to T2 (prompt in-person review), NOT an urgent/emergency tier: verruca vulgaris is a genuine T1-T2 dermatology condition and the acute bleed is a benign, pressure-controllable mechanical haemorrhage in a systemically-well child with a negative secondary-infection screen and no bleeding-diathesis history. The open bleeding lesion, the un-excludable benign vascular mimic (pyogenic granuloma), and the paediatric in-person-review flag justify prompt in-person review over pure reassurance and drive prominent mandatory escalation edges (uncontrolled bleeding after full pressure -> T4 ED for cautery; secondary infection -> T3 same-day; systemic compromise / torrential bleeding -> T5/000) — but not a baseline uplift. minimum_viable_tier_for_pass set to T1 so a correct coach-haemostasis + reassure + routine-GP + explicit safety-net answer PASSES; same-day (T3) review is an acceptable more-cautious alternative. Reflexive escalation to 000/ED (T4/T5) for a controllable bleed, and pure reassurance with no haemostasis coaching/safety-net, are both scored errors.
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00299 — maintainer to confirm/reassign the SEQ within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes recorded for the primary diagnosis and the differentials with verification_status unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. All are best-effort candidates for later terminology-receipt confirmation.

No AMT/PBS drug codes were assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects. No dose was synthesised for the paediatric patient.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
